title: Celsia arcturus
taxonomy: Celsia arcturus is a plant species within the family Plantaginaceae. It belongs to the genus Celsia, a group of plants commonly known as the foxglove or jay's beard. The Plantaginaceae family is a large group of flowering plants that are primarily herbaceous and often found in temperate regions. The classification of Celsia arcturus is part of the broader taxonomy of angiosperms, which includes flowering plants. This classification helps to understand the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Celsia arcturus and other related species.
